messages.content: Hey everyone! 😊 I was just thinking about some ridiculous things I did as a kid—anyone else have funny childhood stories to share? I could use a good laugh today!

Oh man, I have a ton 😅 One time I tried to dig a hole to China in my backyard with a plastic spoon. Got like, six inches deep and was absolutely convinced I’d pop out on the other side by dinner. My mom still brings it up at family gatherings!

LOL Frank, I totally did the same thing but with a *stick* instead of a spoon 😂 Except I got distracted halfway and tried to "cook" mud pies instead. My mom pretended to eat one and I was SO proud. Kids are wild!

These stories are hilarious 😂 Honestly, I was the super cautious kid—while everyone else was digging up the yard or climbing trees, I was inside trying to convince my siblings to play board games with me. Monopoly, Clue, you name it! Still prefer board games over outdoor adventures tbh. Anyone else have a favorite childhood game?

Eve, I was always jealous of the board game families! My crew could never finish a game without someone flipping the board or claiming someone was cheating 😂 I think the only one we ever completed was Candy Land because it was impossible to mess up.

Omg, Frank, Candy Land was a classic! But I totally get the chaos—my siblings were notorious for “accidentally” bumping the board if things weren’t going their way. I still have trust issues with anyone volunteering to be the banker in Monopoly 😂 Did anyone else have those weird made-up house rules or was that just us?

Haha Eve, our Monopoly “rules” were basically chaos too. My brother claimed free parking was a jackpot spot and would slip extra cash under there when no one was looking. I didn’t catch on for *years* 😂 Also, did anyone else make up their own games entirely? My cousins and I invented this epic hide and seek version where you could “freeze” people if you tagged them, and it always ended with us running wild through the house until someone got hurt or something broke!

Alice, we totally made up our own games too! My cousins and I invented something called “lava monster” where the living room floor was lava and you had to leap from couch to chair to table. I’m honestly shocked none of us broke a bone 😂 Also, shoutout to all the sneaky Monopoly bankers out there… I see you!

Grace, “lava monster” was a staple in my house too 😂 My little brother always took it way too seriously and would yell “YOU TOUCHED THE FLOOR, CHARLIE, YOU’RE OUT!” even if I just barely grazed it. Also, I totally remember trying to build “forts” out of couch cushions and getting mad when they kept collapsing. The living room was basically an obstacle course for like five years straight!

Charlie, forts out of couch cushions were the *best*—until you realized someone used the good throw blanket at the bottom and the whole thing collapsed 😂 I swear, the amount of times we’d beg my mom for more “building materials” and she’d just hand us a random towel. Also, did anyone else ever try to set up a “store” and sell completely useless stuff to their parents? I think my mom still has the “artwork” I tried to sell her for a

Alice, yes!! I definitely set up a “store” in the living room once and tried to sell my parents things they already owned—like, “Would you like to buy this remote for a quarter?” 😂 My mom was my best customer but my dad just wanted his stuff back. I even made little price tags with crayons. Entrepreneurial spirit or just desperate for snack money? 😅

Frank, I totally did the same thing! Except I tried to “sell” my sister’s toys back to her, and she actually paid me in jelly beans once 😂 I remember making a whole menu for an imaginary restaurant and serving my parents cereal and water. Honestly, they deserved an award for pretending it was gourmet. Anyone else have parents that went along with your wildest schemes just to keep the peace?

Jack, my mom was a total champ at pretending too 😂 She’d sit down at my “restaurant” and act like a bowl of dry Cheerios was the fanciest thing ever. I even made her write fake reviews in my “guest book” (aka a spiral notebook I stole from school). Looking back, parents really deserve medals for surviving all those weird little kid businesses and games!

Henry, totally agree—parents had *infinite* patience! My dad once let me and my sister put on a magic show for him, and we only knew, like, two tricks. He acted amazed every time I “magically” made a coin disappear behind my ear (it was always behind my ear 😆). I think he deserves an Oscar for that performance, honestly.

Haha Frank, the magic show memories are real! I used to “levitate” my little brother by making him lie on the couch and dramatically count down while I pretended to cast a spell. He was SO convinced the couch was actually floating 😂 And don’t even get me started on puppet shows—we’d use socks and perform for anyone who’d sit still long enough. Honestly, siblings are the most patient audience sometimes!

Alice, omg yes to the sock puppet shows 😂 I used to recruit my cat as the “special guest” and she’d just lay there completely unimpressed while I tried to narrate her “lines.” My parents definitely deserve a medal for sitting through my 30-minute epic about a heroic potato and his sidekick carrot. Looking back, I’m slightly amazed at how much nonsense they put up with! Anyone else have wildly creative (but totally chaotic) shows or games?

Iris, a heroic potato and sidekick carrot is honestly iconic 😂 I used to force my brother to be part of my “TV shows”—I’d make up commercials for fake products and he had to pretend to be the happy customer. One time I convinced him to do a shampoo commercial and poured half a bottle of conditioner on his head… my mom was NOT thrilled. But the commitment to the role was impressive! Did anyone else have “commercial breaks” during their homemade shows?

Eve, yes!! We definitely had commercial breaks 😂 I’d interrupt our “programming” to sell random stuff like “Magic Pencils” (which were just regular pencils with stickers on them) and try to convince my siblings they’d write better if they bought one. My sister would roll her eyes so hard, but sometimes she’d play along just to see how ridiculous I’d get. Looking back, it’s wild how much effort we put into turning the living room into a full-blown

theater production! I swear, every cushion, blanket, and laundry basket became a prop or set piece at some point. Sometimes I’d even make tickets and try to charge my parents “admission” (usually in the form of snacks or spare change). Honestly, I kind of miss how creative we got just to entertain ourselves. Kids these days have iPads, but can an iPad turn into a pirate ship *and* a stage in one afternoon? I think not 😆
Alice, so true! There’s just something about turning your whole house into a different worl

needle_message: These stories are hilarious 😂 Honestly, I was the super cautious kid—while everyone else was digging up the yard or climbing trees, I was inside trying to convince my siblings to play board games with me. Monopoly, Clue, you name it! Still prefer board games over outdoor adventures tbh. Anyone else have a favorite childhood game?
